findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Node.js filesystem access that could read or write sensitive data
Node.js filesystem access that could read or write sensitive data
Indirect Prompt Injection via External Data Source
findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read and embed JSON data from `/tmp/session-report.json` into the HTML template. This JSON is generated by an external script and could contain malicious content that, when embedded, manipulates the agent's behavior or the rendered report. The skill also instructs the agent to fill specific HTML blocks (`<!-- AGENT: anomalies -->`, `<!-- AGENT: optimizations -->`) based on this external data, creating a vector for indirect prompt injection.
findings.evidence: The skill reads `/tmp/session-report.json` and uses its content to generate narrative findings and optimizations. This is a classic indirect prompt injection pattern where untrusted external data influences agent output.
findings.remediation: Validate and sanitize the JSON data before embedding. Do not automatically generate narrative content from external data without user review. Consider using a fixed template that only displays raw data.

findings.title: Data Exfiltration via Tool Chaining
findings.description: The skill chains multiple tools to read sensitive session data from `~/.claude/projects` and write it to a publicly accessible HTML file in the current working directory. The script `analyze-sessions.mjs` reads transcripts from `~/.claude/projects`, which may contain sensitive information including API keys, user prompts, and system interactions. This data is then embedded into an HTML file that could be accidentally shared or exposed.
findings.evidence: Step 1 runs a script that reads from `~/.claude/projects`. Step 4 embeds the full JSON output into an HTML file. This creates a data flow from sensitive local storage to a portable file.
findings.remediation: Add warnings about sensitive data exposure. Consider redacting or filtering sensitive fields before embedding. Require explicit user confirmation before saving the report.

findings.title: Tool Exploitation - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill's manifest does not declare `allowed-tools`, but the instructions require the agent to use Bash (to run the analyzer script and copy files), Edit (to modify the HTML template), and potentially Read (to read the JSON output). Without explicit tool restrictions, this is a LOW severity issue, but the skill's behavior involves multiple tool operations that should be declared.
findings.evidence: The YAML frontmatter has no `allowed-tools` field. The instructions require `Bash`, `Edit`, and `Read` operations.
findings.remediation: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, Edit, Read, Write]` to the YAML frontmatter to explicitly declare the required tools.

findings.title: Command Injection Risk via User-Controlled Input
findings.description: The skill accepts user input for the time range (e.g., `24h`, `30d`, `all`) and passes it directly to a shell command without sanitization. The instruction says 'honor a different range if the user passed one', which means user input is interpolated into the command `node <skill-dir>/analyze-sessions.mjs --json --since <user_input>`. This could allow command injection if the user provides malicious input.
findings.evidence: The skill dynamically constructs a shell command with user-provided input: `node <skill-dir>/analyze-sessions.mjs --json --since 7d`. The instruction says to honor a different range if the user passed one, implying user input is used directly.
findings.remediation: Validate and sanitize user input for the time range. Use a whitelist of allowed values (e.g., `24h`, `7d`, `30d`, `all`). Do not pass user input directly to shell commands.

findings.title: Detection Evasion - Obfuscated Script Execution
findings.description: The skill references a bundled script `analyze-sessions.mjs` that is not provided for analysis. This script could contain obfuscated or malicious code that evades detection. The skill's instructions assume the script is benign, but without examining the script, its true behavior cannot be verified.
findings.evidence: The skill runs `node <skill-dir>/analyze-sessions.mjs --json --since 7d` but the script file is not included in the analysis. This creates a blind spot for security review.
findings.remediation: Include the script file for analysis. If the script cannot be provided, document its behavior explicitly and consider using a simpler, verifiable approach.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Over-broad Description
findings.description: The skill description claims to 'Generate an explorable HTML report of Claude Code session usage' but the actual behavior involves reading sensitive session data, running external scripts, and modifying HTML templates. The description understates the scope of operations and data access required.
findings.evidence: Description: 'Generate an explorable HTML report of Claude Code session usage'. Actual behavior: runs external script, reads sensitive data, modifies HTML files, generates narrative content.
findings.remediation: Update the description to accurately reflect all operations: 'Analyze Claude Code session transcripts, generate a data-driven HTML report with narrative findings, and save it to the current directory.'
